title: SonicWall SSL-VPN Concurrent Sessions vs Licensed Capacity (SonicWall)
dataSources: `sourcetype=dell:sonicwall` management or SSL-VPN session summary events
spl:
index=netops sourcetype="dell:sonicwall" earliest=-4h
| where match(_raw,"(?i)ssl-vpn|NetExtender|session")
| rex field=_raw "(?i)active[\s_]*sessions[\s:=]+(?<sessions>\d+)"
| stats latest(sessions) as active_sessions by host
| eval license_limit=250
| eval pct=round(100*active_sessions/license_limit,1)
| where pct>=80
description: VPN license exhaustion blocks remote workers during incidents or weather events. Session counts should be tracked like any other capacity metric.
value: Prevents emergency license purchases and documents growth for budget planning.
implementation: Enable SSL-VPN session logging. If only available via SNMP/API, ingest via scripted input into `sourcetype=sonicwall:vpn:metrics`.
visualization: Single value (sessions vs cap), timechart (concurrent sessions).

references.title: Splunkbase — Dell SonicWall Add-on for Splunk
Splunk Connect for Syslog — Dell SonicWall reference

grandmaExplanation: VPN license exhaustion blocks remote workers during incidents or weather events — we monitor SonicWall logs so teams can act before users feel pain.
